hooks.name: Session start — agent directory pointer
hooks.description: Reminds the assistant on session start that .kiro/agents/ holds the specialized sub-agents for the @stackra/* framework + Academorix frontend monorepo, and that multi-step work should prefer invoke_sub_agent over inline work.

action.prompt:
.kiro/agents/ holds the specialized sub-agents for this repo — one Markdown file per agent documents its lane, tools, and rules.

For any multi-step work that fits one of these lanes, prefer invoke_sub_agent over doing it inline:

## Reviewers (READ-ONLY audits, produce reports, never modify code)
- container-di-architecture-reviewer — DI graph, module lifecycle, decorators, discovery, React bindings for @stackra/container and the non-UI packages.
- package-api-release-reviewer — subpath exports, dual ESM/CJS tsup builds, tree-shaking, pnpm catalogs, Changesets/semver, HeroUI Pro licensed-postinstall supply-chain surface.
- ui-design-a11y-reviewer — HeroUI design-taste conformance, theming/token consistency, accessibility for @stackra/ui and the vite-example app.

## Writers (bounded — one lane, one kind of edit)
- framework-core-builder — writes source for the non-UI @stackra/* packages (container, contracts, events, logger, support, cache, http, queue, scheduler, pipeline, realtime, ssr, collaboration, coordinator).
- heroui-ui-builder — writes React/HeroUI UI code inside @stackra/ui and the vite-example app, using @heroui/react + @heroui-pro/react.
- code-standards-steward — enforces one-export-per-file, suffix-per-kind naming, folder-per-category taxonomy, index barrels. Moves/splits/renames sources only; never changes behaviour.
- code-documentation-writer — writes top-of-file @file/@module docblocks and JSDoc on every public export. Never changes code semantics.
- support-utilities-steward — audits or migrates native calls (.toLowerCase(), process.env.X, hand-rolled sleep/retry) to canonical @stackra/support helpers (Str, Env, sleep, retry, once, ...).
- workspace-standardization-steward — normalizes package.json, tsconfig.json, tsup.config.ts, vitest.config.ts, exports maps. Manifests only; never source, tests, README, or changesets.
- docs-changesets-steward — writes root README, per-package READMEs, changesets, CHANGELOGs, steering. Never source code.
- vitest-test-engineer — writes and improves Vitest test suites (with @stackra/testing preset, RTL for UI packages). Tests only.
- translator — audits + scaffolds per-package frontend i18n catalogs: two JSON files per package (en.json + ar.json) at packages/<pkg>/src/core/i18n/, following the .ref/packages/i18n/ reference runtime's Vite plugin discovery convention. NO TypeScript, NO interfaces, NO register helpers, NO barrel edits. Arabic is machine-generated and flagged in the scaffold report for a native Arabic-speaking reviewer pass before ship.

## Advisory (no code, no writes)
- academorix-product — enterprise product lead (PM/PO/BA hat) for domain modelling, INVEST user stories, business-type strategy (Academy/SportsCenter/School/Gym/Federation/Club), enterprise readiness (SSO/SCIM/DR), and compliance reasoning (GDPR, FERPA, COPPA, CCPA, PCI-DSS, WCAG 2.2 AA, SOC 2, ISO 27001).

Single-file edits, direct questions, and small in-scope changes stay inline.

hooks.name: Session start — no for/while loops in zsh tool commands
hooks.description: Reminds the assistant that shell commands passed to execute_bash / control_bash_process run under macOS zsh, where for/while one-liners are fragile. Use dedicated tools, xargs, find -exec, pnpm -r, or parallel tool calls instead.

action.prompt:
Shell guardrail (macOS zsh): NEVER put `for` or `while` loops in commands you hand to `execute_bash` or `control_bash_process`.

Why: zsh does NOT word-split unquoted `$var` or `$(cmd)` the way bash does, so `for f in $(ls *.ts)` iterates ONCE over the whole space-joined string instead of once per file. Failures inside `; do ... ; done` one-liners get silently swallowed. Interactive `read`-driven `while` loops hang until timeout.

Use instead:
- Dedicated tools first: file_search, grep_search, read_files, list_directory — they return structured results.
- `xargs` / `find -exec` for one-shot fan-out.
- `pnpm -r` or `pnpm --filter=<pkg>` for anything that spans workspace packages.
- Separate parallel tool calls when operations are independent — the tool driver reports each output separately.

Applies ONLY to tool-invoked command strings (execute_bash, control_bash_process, hook action.command values). Authored `.sh` scripts committed to the repo are exempt — ordinary scripting rules apply.
